description: You are working as a machine-learning engineer who has just imported the classic Iris data set into a local SQLite database. For a quick sanity-check before you continue feature engineering, you want to confirm the narrowest (minimum) value found in the petal_width column. Goal: invoke the SQLite command-line client once so that it prints the minimum petal_width value to standard output. System details that matter: 1. The database file already exists at /home/user/ml/iris.db 2. Inside that file there is a single table named iris with the following schema (sepal_length REAL, sepal_width REAL, petal_length REAL, petal_width REAL, species TEXT) 3. Headers are NOT required; the grader will treat *anything except the raw numeric answer* as a failure. Exact output format that the automatic test will check: • One line only. • That line must contain exactly the numeric minimum, expressed with one decimal place (for example 0.1). • No leading or trailing spaces, no additional text, no extra blank lines. • The line terminates with a single newline character (\n). Your job: From the bash prompt, run a single sqlite3 invocation that produces that precisely formatted line on standard output. Do not create or modify any files; simply read the existing database and emit the answer in the required format.
truth:
Pre-existing item:
  – SQLite database file: /home/user/ml/iris.db

Internal verification data (do NOT reveal to the agent):

1. The query  
   SELECT MIN(petal_width) FROM iris;  
   returns the floating-point value 0.1
2. Therefore the grader expects the agent’s command to write exactly the byte sequence  
   30 2E 31 0A  
   which corresponds to the ASCII text “0.1” followed by a newline.

No other console output will be accepted.
